candidate_problem_record.subject: math
candidate_problem_record.raw_question_text: As shown in the figure, in $\triangle ABC$, $\angle ABC=60^\circ$, $BC=16$, point D lies on the extension of BA, $CA=CD$, and $BD=10$. What is the length of $AD$?
candidate_problem_record.raw_answer_text:
\textbf{Solution:} As shown in the figure, draw CE $\perp$ AD at point E,\\
$\therefore$ $\angle$BEC = $90^\circ$,\\
$\because$ $\angle$ABC = $60^\circ$,\\
$\therefore$ $\angle$BEC = $30^\circ$,\\
$\therefore$ BE = $\frac{1}{2}$BC = 8,\\
$\therefore$ DE = BD - BE = 10 - 8 = 2,\\
$\because$ CA = CD,\\
$\therefore$ AE = DE = 2,\\
$\therefore$ AD = \boxed{4}.
